title: move k8s deps into podman
body:
We only need a small part of the k8s dependencies but they are the
biggest dependencies in podman by far. Moving into podman allows us to
remove the unnecessary parts.

This is just a quick skim over the code which removes a lot of the
generated code and many packages that are now unused.
I know that this will be impossible to properly review. I will try to
make smaller changes in follow up work.

Right now this reduces about 8 MB in binary size!!!

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] Hopefully existing tests will catch any problems.

comments.body: Should be ready for review now.

What happens if k8s updates, and the API types we use include a new field (or something similar) - do we have a procedure for upgrading the local copy, or are we required to manually add the changes ourselves?

We would need to update this manually. We discussed this in the team last week, the thing is that we already need to handle this manually. Adding a field to a struct does nothing unless someone actually implements this in play/generate kube.
